message.content:
| Aspect | LLC | S-Corp | C-Corp |
|--------|-----|--------|--------|
| **Taxation** | Pass-through: profits/losses to owners' personal returns (default); can elect corp tax | Pass-through: profits/losses to shareholders' personal returns (avoids double tax) | Double taxation: corp-level (21% federal) + dividends to shareholders taxed personally |
| **Ownership** | Unlimited members; any individuals/entities; no share classes | ≤100 shareholders; U.S. citizens/residents only; one class of stock | Unlimited shareholders; any individuals/entities; multiple stock classes allowed |
| **Management** | Member-managed or manager-managed (flexible) | Board of directors + officers required | Board of directors + officers required |
| **Paperwork/Formation** | Articles of organization + operating agreement (simple, state-filed) | Articles of incorporation + bylaws + IRS Form 2553 election (more formal) | Articles of incorporation + bylaws (most formal, ongoing compliance) |
| **Ongoing Compliance** | Minimal: annual reports, state fees | Moderate: annual reports, payroll taxes, shareholder consents | High: annual reports, board minutes, shareholder meetings, SEC filings if public |
| **Liability Protection** | Strong personal asset protection | Strong (as corp) + pass-through tax | Strong personal asset protection |
| **Pros** | Flexible, simple setup, pass-through tax, self-employment tax savings via distributions | Pass-through tax, self-employment tax savings on distributions, perpetual existence | Unlimited growth potential, stock options, attract investors, deduct benefits |
| **Cons** | Self-employment taxes on all income (unless elect S/C), state fees vary | Ownership/share restrictions, strict IRS rules, payroll complexity | Double taxation, heavy compliance, less tax flexibility |
| **Best For** | Solos/small teams wanting simplicity/flexibility | Owner-employees seeking tax savings on distributions | Scaling businesses/VC funding needing stock incentives |
| **Conversion Ease** | Easy to elect S/C status | Can convert from LLC; hard to C-corp | Difficult to change without tax hits |

Note: Consult a tax advisor for your situation—rules vary by state and change (e.g., as of 2023). This is a high-level overview to aid quick decisions.
